items.headline: Russia unleashes massive missile and drone attack on Kyiv
items.body: Russia launched one of the war's largest air attacks on Ukraine's capital city. [8, 9] The assault killed dozens of civilians and destroyed a nine-story apartment block. [6, 9]

items.headline: African leaders push to process critical minerals on the continent
items.body: A summit backed a new guarantee mechanism to unlock private investment for local manufacturing. [20, 31] This shifts economic strategy from exporting raw materials to creating domestic value chains. [15]

items.headline: Samsung Electronics faces imminent strike after labor talks collapse
items.body: Last-ditch wage negotiations between the company and its largest union have failed. [7, 12, 13] The government is considering emergency arbitration to avert major disruption to chip production. [23, 25, 26]

items.headline: Trump and Xi agree on three-year strategic stability framework during Beijing summit
items.body: The US and China committed to a named framework governing relations through 2029. China will buy more American oil starting this quarter.

items.headline: Philippine Senate installs new president hours before second Duterte impeachment
items.body: Thirteen senators voted Cayetano into power, replacing Sotto. The impeachment court convenes May 18 to try the sitting vice president.

items.headline: Latvian government collapses after coalition partner withdraws over drone response
items.body: Prime Minister Silina resigned after losing majority support. Multiple Ukrainian drones have crashed in Latvia since March without interception.

items.headline: Ghana evacuates 300 citizens from South Africa amid xenophobic attacks
items.body: Foreign Ministry confirmed evacuation flights have begun for registered citizens. Nigeria reports 130 citizens have requested repatriation.
